questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite angle $\theta$ is 5 cm and the hypotenuse is 13 cm. What is the value of sin($\theta$)?
questions.explanation: The sine of an angle is the ratio of the length of the opposite side to the length of the hypotenuse (SOH). So, sin($\theta$) = Opposite / Hypotenuse = 5/13.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the side adjacent to angle $\theta$ is 8 cm and the hypotenuse is 17 cm. What is the value of cos($\theta$)?
questions.explanation: The cosine of an angle is the ratio of the length of the adjacent side to the length of the hypotenuse (CAH). So, cos($\theta$) = Adjacent / Hypotenuse = 8/17.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite angle $\theta$ is 9 cm and the adjacent side is 40 cm. What is the value of tan($\theta$)?
questions.explanation: The tangent of an angle is the ratio of the length of the opposite side to the length of the adjacent side (TOA). So, tan($\theta$) = Opposite / Adjacent = 9/40.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, one angle is 30° and the hypotenuse is 18 cm. What is the length of the side opposite the 30° angle?
questions.explanation: Use the sine ratio: sin(angle) = Opposite / Hypotenuse. So, sin(30°) = Opposite / 18. Opposite = 18 * sin(30°) = 18 * 0.5 = 9 cm.

questions.questionText: A right-angled triangle has an angle of 60° and a hypotenuse of 14 cm. Calculate the length of the side adjacent to the 60° angle.
questions.explanation: Use the cosine ratio: cos(angle) = Adjacent / Hypotenuse. So, cos(60°) = Adjacent / 14. Adjacent = 14 * cos(60°) = 14 * 0.5 = 7 cm.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite an angle of 40° is 10 cm long. What is the length of the hypotenuse, correct to one decimal place?
questions.explanation: Use the sine ratio: sin(angle) = Opposite / Hypotenuse. So, sin(40°) = 10 / Hypotenuse. Hypotenuse = 10 / sin(40°) ≈ 15.6 cm.

questions.questionText: The side opposite angle A in a right-angled triangle is 7 cm and the hypotenuse is 10 cm. Find the size of angle A, correct to one decimal place.
questions.explanation: Use the sine ratio: sin(A) = Opposite / Hypotenuse = 7/10 = 0.7. To find the angle, use the inverse sine function: A = $sin^{-1}$(0.7) ≈ 44.4°.

questions.questionText: The side adjacent to angle B in a right-angled triangle is 5 cm and the hypotenuse is 13 cm. Find the size of angle B, correct to one decimal place.
questions.explanation: Use the cosine ratio: cos(B) = Adjacent / Hypotenuse = 5/13. To find the angle, use the inverse cosine function: B = $cos^{-1}$(5/13) ≈ 67.4°.

questions.questionText: The side opposite angle C is 8 cm and the adjacent side is 11 cm. Find the size of angle C in this right-angled triangle, correct to one decimal place.
questions.explanation: Use the tangent ratio: tan(C) = Opposite / Adjacent = 8/11. To find the angle, use the inverse tangent function: C = $tan^{-1}$(8/11) ≈ 36.0°.

questions.questionText: A ladder of length 5 m leans against a vertical wall. The foot of the ladder is 3 m from the base of the wall. What is the angle the ladder makes with the ground, to one decimal place?
questions.explanation: The ladder, wall, and ground form a right-angled triangle. The ladder is the hypotenuse (5 m) and the distance from the wall is the adjacent side (3 m). Use cos($\theta$) = Adjacent / Hypotenuse = 3/5. $\theta = cos^{-1}$(0.6) ≈ 53.1°.

questions.questionText: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite an angle of 50° is 12 cm. What is the length of the adjacent side, correct to one decimal place?
questions.explanation: Use the tangent ratio: tan(angle) = Opposite / Adjacent. So, tan(50°) = 12 / Adjacent. Adjacent = 12 / tan(50°) ≈ 10.1 cm.

questions.questionText: A right-angled triangle has a hypotenuse of 20 cm. An angle in the triangle is 25°. What is the length of the side ADJACENT to this angle, correct to one decimal place?
questions.explanation: To find the adjacent side, use the cosine ratio (CAH). cos(25°) = Adjacent / 20. So, Adjacent = 20 * cos(25°) ≈ 18.1 cm. Using sine would incorrectly give the opposite side.
